rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND PARKER-GAYLORD VS. DEUTSCHE BANK, NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA RECONVEYANCE COMPANY and JPMORGAN CHASE BANK's AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED without leave to amend in part and OVERRULED in part. The demurrer is OVERRULED was to Cause of Action 5, promissory estoppel. Plaintiff's complaint makes out a valid claim for promissory estoppel and factual issues exist with regard to the nature of Defense Counsel's promise and his ability to bind the foreclosing party that preclude sustaining the demurrer. The demurrer is SUSTAINED without leave to amend on all other causes of action. Defendants were not the foreclosing parties, and Plaintiff has not alleged prejudice stemming from his allegations that the wrong party is foreclosing on the debt he admits he owes. Furthermore, the Homeowner's Bill of Rights is not retroactive and therefore does not apply to this case. (501/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: MINHALL, INC.A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. RICHARD A. CHRISTENSEN, AN INDIVIDUAL, DOING et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF MINHALL, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication: CONTINUED to November 14, 2014 at 9:30 AM in Department 501. Moving party failed to provide a courtesy copy of its moving papers, in violation of Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party is ordered to provide a courtesy copy of its papers by November 3, 2014 or the motion will be taken off calendar. (501/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: T.D. SERVICE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT JAMIE LEE's SPECIAL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED. Plaintiff has not submitted evidence showing that the bankruptcy stay, filed in this court on May 1, 2014, has been vacated or does not apply to the surplus proceeds at issue. (501/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: T.D. SERVICE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Quashing Summons And Dismissal For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT JAMIE LEE's Motion For Order Quashing Summons And Dismissal For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction: Defendant's Motion to Quash / Dismiss is MOOT given the Court's ruling on the Special Demurrer. (501/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN TSANG VS. CHARLES ENGELBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Overrule Objections And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Requests For Admissions, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF ELLEN TSANG's Motion To Overrule Objections And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions, And For Monetary Sanctions: GRANTED. Defendant failed to provide substantive, good-faith responses or objections. For example, Defendant responded to the question "state your date and place of birth" with a 10 sentence objection including claims that the information was protected by the attorney-client privilege, that it was vague and ambiguous, that it violated the defendant's constitutional right to privacy, that it assumed facts not in evidence, and that it was unintelligible under the circumstances. Defendant is ordered to provide substantive, good-faith responses by November 3, 2014. Sanctions of $60 are GRANTED. (501/LOT)

rulings.Case Title: RAUL UCAN ET AL VS. 270 TURK , LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014 line 8. DEFENDANTS 270 TURK , LLC, VERITAS INVESTMENTS, INC, YAT-PANG AU and GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC 's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The complaint adequately pleads that Plaintiff Mena was a tenant at the property and Defendants' allegations to the contrary represent a dispute of material fact not suitable to resolution on demurrer. = (501/LOT)
